name: Tunis
tagline: Mediterranean Charm, French-Arab Culture, and Incredible Value on the North African Coast
description: Tunis blends Mediterranean sophistication with North African warmth and affordability. The ancient medina is a UNESCO World Heritage Site, the ruins of Carthage sit on the city's outskirts, and the beaches of La Marsa and Sidi Bou Said are among the most beautiful in the Mediterranean. French colonial architecture, excellent cuisine fusing Arab and French traditions, and a mild Mediterranean climate make Tunis a hidden gem for FIRE retirees. Costs are remarkably low, French is essential (English is limited), and the expat community is small but growing.

quickFacts.visa: 90-day visa-free stay for US citizens; renewable residence permits available
quickFacts.safetyRating: Moderate to Good – generally safe in tourist and residential areas
quickFacts.climate: Mediterranean with hot dry summers and mild wet winters
quickFacts.englishFriendly: Low – French is essential; Arabic helpful

housing.description: Spacious 2BR apartment in La Marsa, Carthage, or Les Berges du Lac with sea views

dining.description: Regular dining at local restaurants serving couscous, brik, and grilled fish; French-style cafes daily

healthcare.description: Private health insurance with access to Clinique La Marsa and other private clinics

transportation.description: TGM light rail and taxis; occasional Bolt rides; no car needed in central areas

entertainment.description: Carthage ruins, Sidi Bou Said village, medina exploration, beach days, cafe culture

1M.lifestyle: Tunis at $3,333/month is extraordinarily comfortable. You can live in a lovely apartment near the Mediterranean, dine out regularly on incredible Tunisian-French fusion cuisine, and explore ancient ruins and blue-and-white coastal villages at your leisure. The surplus allows for significant savings or European travel. Learning French is strongly recommended.

housing.description: Beautiful 3BR villa in La Marsa, Gammarth, or Sidi Bou Said with garden and sea views

dining.description: Regular dining at Tunis's best restaurants like Dar El Jeld, La Closerie, and coastal seafood spots

healthcare.description: Comprehensive private insurance with dental, specialist, and international evacuation coverage

transportation.description: Reliable car with insurance for exploring Tunisia; fuel is cheap

entertainment.description: Golf at La Cigale, Carthage International Festival, sailing, spa hammam, Djerba trips, Sahara excursions

2M.lifestyle: At $6,667/month Tunis becomes a Mediterranean paradise at a fraction of European prices. A villa with sea views, domestic help, regular dining at the city's finest restaurants, and the freedom to explore Tunisia's extraordinary diversity from Mediterranean beaches to Saharan oases. Weekday cafe culture and weekend adventures make for an incredibly fulfilling rhythm.

housing.description: Luxury seafront villa in Gammarth, La Marsa, or a restored traditional house in the medina

dining.description: Fine dining at the best restaurants, private dinner parties with Tunisian and French cuisine

healthcare.description: Premium private insurance with international coverage and medical evacuation to Europe

transportation.description: Late-model car, insurance, occasional private driver, domestic flights

entertainment.description: Golf and tennis clubs, sailing, Carthage Festival VIP, luxury Sahara camps, European weekend getaways

3M.lifestyle: At $10K/month in Tunis you are living at the very top of local society. A stunning seafront villa, a full-time cook preparing fresh Tunisian cuisine, luxury desert adventures, and easy flights to Paris, Rome, or Barcelona whenever you want a European fix. The blend of African, Arab, and Mediterranean cultures creates a daily experience of extraordinary richness.

housing.description: Grand seaside estate in Gammarth or Sidi Bou Said with pool, gardens, and panoramic views

dining.description: Personal chef specializing in Tunisian-French cuisine, exclusive dining, catered events

healthcare.description: Top-tier international insurance with concierge service and European medical evacuation

transportation.description: Luxury vehicle, full-time driver, frequent flights to European capitals

entertainment.description: Exclusive club memberships, sailing, cultural patronage, art collecting, international festivals

domesticHelp.description: Full household staff: housekeeper, chef, gardener, security, driver, personal assistant

luxuryExtras.description: First-class European travel, private Sahara lodges, real estate investments, philanthropy

5M.lifestyle: Tunis at $16,667/month is palatial Mediterranean living at African prices. A grand seaside estate with a full staff, a personal chef, first-class flights to Europe, and luxury desert adventures. You are living like a Mediterranean aristocrat for what would barely cover a modest apartment in the south of France.

housing.description: Palatial seaside compound in Gammarth or Sidi Bou Said with guest houses and panoramic Mediterranean views

dining.description: Personal chef team, exclusive private dining, catered events for international guests

healthcare.description: Top-tier international concierge medicine, executive wellness, European hospital access

transportation.description: Multiple luxury vehicles, full-time driver team, private charter flights

entertainment.description: Major cultural patronage, international art collecting, yacht charter, global philanthropy

domesticHelp.description: Full estate staff: multiple housekeepers, chef team, security detail, drivers, personal assistant, property manager

luxuryExtras.description: Yacht, real estate portfolio, first-class global travel, major educational philanthropy, cultural sponsorship

10M.lifestyle: At $33K/month in Tunis you are living a life of extraordinary grandeur on the Mediterranean. A palatial compound overlooking the sea where Carthage once stood, a full estate staff, a yacht, and the resources to fund meaningful change across Tunisia. The surplus at this level is enormous, enabling transformative philanthropy, real estate investment, and a global travel schedule that spans continents.
